title: On the geometry of uniform meandric systems
speaker: Ewain Gwynne
abstract: A meandric system of size $n$ is the set of loops formed from two arc diagrams (non-crossing perfect matchings) on $\{1,\dots,2n\}$, one drawn above the real line and the other below the real line. Equivalently, a meandric system is a coupled collection of meanders of total size $n$. I will discuss a conjecture which describes the large-scale geometry of a uniformly sampled meandric system of size $n$ in terms of Liouville quantum gravity (LQG) decorated by certain Schramm-Loewner evolution (SLE) type curves.

proof_stages.label: Meandric systems and their Dyck-path encoding
proof_stages.summary: The talk defines meanders and meandric systems modulo line-preserving homeomorphism, then establishes the key bijection between meandric systems, pairs of noncrossing matchings, and pairs of Dyck-path excursions, giving Catalan enumeration and a uniform sampling method.

proof_stages.label: Geometric questions and prior bounds
proof_stages.summary: The speaker poses the main geometric questions for a uniform meandric system--number of loops, largest-loop size, possible dominance by one loop, and scaling limits--and reviews known results such as linear loop count, logarithmic prior lower bounds, and numerical predictions.

proof_stages.label: Planar-map scaling-limit conjecture
proof_stages.summary: A meandric system is reformulated as a planar map decorated by a Hamiltonian path and loops, leading to the conjecture that the scaling limit is sqrt(2)-Liouville quantum gravity decorated by an SLE8 path and an independent CLE6 loop ensemble.

proof_stages.label: Heuristic support and KPZ exponent predictions
proof_stages.summary: The scaling conjecture is justified via the mating-of-trees/random-walk encoding, physics heuristics of Di Francesco-Golinelli-Guitter, and numerical simulations, and it yields a KPZ prediction that each fixed-rank largest loop has size n^{alpha+o(1)} with alpha about 0.79.

proof_stages.label: Finite-volume macroscopic-loop theorem
proof_stages.summary: Using the LQG metric dimension d and comparison to mated-CRT-map techniques, plus a discrete parity trick, the speaker states that the largest loop has graph-distance diameter n^{1/d+o(1)}, hence at least n^{1/d+o(1)} vertices.

proof_stages.label: Infinite meandric systems and the no-noodle problem
proof_stages.summary: The uniform infinite meandric system is defined through local limits or bi-infinite random walks, the uniqueness and conjectured nonexistence of an infinite noodle are reviewed, and a half-plane variant is proved to have no infinite paths and infinitely many loops disconnecting the origin from infinity.

proof_stages.label: Percolation-interface reinterpretation
proof_stages.summary: By alternately coloring half-integers and connecting same-colored regions not separated by meandric loops, the speaker interprets the loops as interfaces between open and closed clusters, aligning macroscopic-loop and no-infinite-path questions with critical percolation analogues.
